milestones.prompt: Design the memory layout for a lock-free SPSC ring buffer in POSIX shared memory using shm_open and mmap. Include struct layout, indices, and how producer/consumer coordinate safely.
milestones.milestone_reason_optional: Validates that the model understands low-level shared memory layout and SPSC invariants before coding.
milestones.continuation_criteria: Model defines a correct ring buffer layout with head/tail indices, avoids locks, and explains memory ordering strategy.

planned_interactions_list.trigger: Model proposes using Mutex or channels
planned_interactions_list.reaction: No locks. This must be lock-free using atomics.
Be explicit about Acquire/Release ordering for producer and consumer.

planned_interactions_list.trigger: Model does not separate head/tail to avoid false sharing
planned_interactions_list.reaction: Think about cache-line alignment. Where do head and tail live?

milestones.prompt: Implement the shared memory setup in Rust using libc (shm_open, ftruncate, mmap). No abstraction crates.
milestones.milestone_reason_optional: Ensures correct low-level IPC setup before building the data structure.
milestones.continuation_criteria: Model correctly uses shm_open, sets size with ftruncate, and maps memory with mmap.

planned_interactions_list.trigger: Model uses external crates for shared memory
planned_interactions_list.reaction: Do it via libc only. No wrappers.

planned_interactions_list.trigger: Model forgets to handle mmap failure
planned_interactions_list.reaction: Check return values. mmap can fail.

milestones.prompt: Implement the lock-free SPSC ring buffer operations (enqueue/dequeue) using atomics.
milestones.continuation_criteria: Push/pop implemented using atomic head/tail with correct wraparound and memory ordering.

planned_interactions_list.reaction: Too strong. Use minimal ordering needed.
Check when head/tail is updated relative to data write/read.

milestones.prompt: Create separate producer and consumer binaries that use the shared ring buffer.
milestones.continuation_criteria: Two binaries communicate correctly through shared memory.

planned_interactions_list.trigger: Model runs both in same process

milestones.prompt: Add benchmarking for throughput and latency across multiple buffer sizes.
milestones.continuation_criteria: Benchmark reports throughput/latency for varying capacities.

planned_interactions_list.reaction: Break it into throughput and per-message latency.

milestones.prompt: Compare performance with and without cache-line padding to demonstrate false sharing.
milestones.continuation_criteria: Model shows measurable performance difference and explains it.

planned_interactions_list.trigger: Model does not modify struct layout
planned_interactions_list.reaction: You need two versions: padded vs non-padded.

rubrics.name: r1_memory_layout_correct
rubrics.criterion: Ring buffer uses separate atomic head and tail indices in shared memory.

rubrics.name: r2_no_locks_used
rubrics.criterion: Solution does not use Mutex, RwLock, or any blocking synchronization primitive.

rubrics.name: r3_memory_ordering_defined
rubrics.criterion: Atomic operations use explicit Acquire/Release ordering.

rubrics.name: r4_uses_shm_open
rubrics.criterion: Shared memory is created using shm_open via libc.

rubrics.name: r6_atomic_enqueue_logic
rubrics.criterion: Enqueue operation writes data before advancing head index.

rubrics.name: r7_atomic_dequeue_logic
rubrics.criterion: Dequeue operation reads data before advancing tail index.

rubrics.name: r8_multi_process_execution
rubrics.criterion: Producer and consumer run as separate OS processes.

rubrics.name: r9_benchmark_reports_metrics
rubrics.criterion: Benchmark outputs both throughput and latency.

rubrics.name: r10_false_sharing_experiment
rubrics.criterion: Solution includes both padded and non-padded struct versions for comparison.

rubrics.name: r11_progressive_decomposition
rubrics.criterion: Model breaks problem into design, implementation, and benchmarking stages before coding everything.

rubrics.name: r12_handles_feedback
rubrics.criterion: Model adjusts approach correctly after user correction.

rubrics.name: r13_concise_technical_explanations
rubrics.criterion: Responses are concise and technically precise without unnecessary verbosity.
